message:
franken_numpy-78g9 expose numpy.lib.recfunctions __all__ namespace

Mirror numpy.lib.recfunctions.__all__ verbatim on
fnp_python.lib.recfunctions and lazy-fill any name we don't already
wrap so the full 21-name structured-array helper surface resolves.
When numpy isn't importable at module init, fall back to the canonical
21-name list captured here so callers iterating __all__ still see a
complete surface.

Add recfunctions_all_namespace_matches_numpy parity test:
- __all__ is non-empty and every listed name resolves on our module.
- When numpy is importable, our __all__ equals numpy's verbatim
  (order included) and every name is callable on both sides; local
  wrappers (drop/rename/append/merge/u2s) are permitted to differ
  in identity but must remain callable.
- When numpy is missing, the embedded fallback list still covers every
  one of the canonical 21 names.

rch lib suite: 473/473 pass. UBS exit 0.

 {"id":"franken_numpy-72oe","title":"Expose put/place/put_along_axis in fnp-python with NumPy parity tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-16T21:47:39.212615842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:07:30.906841680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T22:07:30.904044793Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-72v","title":"Scale eigenvalue detection thresholds relative to matrix norm in fnp-linalg","description":"## Problem

In fnp-linalg's eigenvalue/eigenvector computation (eig_nxn_full), multiple absolute tolerance constants are used for different but related purposes:

- Line ~2086: `1e-10` — checks if sub-diagonal element in Schur form exceeds this threshold to identify complex eigenvalue pairs
- Line ~2329: `1e-10` — same check during eigenvector computation for 2×2 blocks
- Line ~2350: `1e-10` — block detection during eigenvector computation
- Line ~2342: `1e-15` — denominator check in eigenvector back-substitution (already partially fixed to use sign-preserving epsilon)
- Line ~2363: `1e-30` — complex denominator threshold

These absolute thresholds fail for matrices with very large or very small entries. A matrix with entries ~1e20 would have sub-diagonal elements ~1e10 that are mathematically zero but exceed the 1e-10 threshold. Conversely, a matrix with entries ~1e-20 might have genuine complex eigenvalues whose sub-diagonal elements are below 1e-10.

## What NumPy/LAPACK Does

LAPACK's DHSEQR and DTREVC routines use relative thresholds:
- `smallnum = DLAMCH('S') / DLAMCH('P')` (safe minimum / precision ≈ 1e-292 / 1.1e-16)
- Sub-diagonal test: `|H(i+1,i)| <= max(eps * (|H(i,i)| + |H(i+1,i+1)|), smlnum)`
- This scales with the diagonal elements, not absolute thresholds

## Proposed Fix

1. Compute matrix 1-norm or Frobenius norm at function entry
2. Define `eps_relative = f64::EPSILON * matrix_norm` (or `f64::EPSILON * max(|diag elements|)`)
3. Replace absolute `1e-10` thresholds with `eps_relative`
4. Replace absolute `1e-15` back-substitution threshold with `f64::EPSILON * matrix_norm`
5. Replace `1e-30` complex denominator threshold with `f64::MIN_POSITIVE`

## Consistency Issue

The current code uses THREE different thresholds (1e-10, 1e-15, 1e-30) for conceptually similar "is this value negligible?" checks. After the fix, all should use a single scale-relative approach.

## Validation

- All 201 fnp-linalg tests must pass
- All 114 conformance tests must pass (especially linalg_differential suite)
- Test with both well-conditioned and ill-conditioned matrices
- Test with matrices having entries of very different magnitudes (1e-10 to 1e10)

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs` — eig_nxn_full function and related eigenvector extraction","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:31.035683392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:12.053559340Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:12.053004790Z","close_reason":"Replaced all 7 absolute thresholds in eig_nxn_full and extract_schur_eigenvalues with scale-relative eps computed from matrix infinity-norm. Added matrix_inf_norm helper. All 201 linalg tests + full workspace pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-72v","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-z8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:48.666572362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Include oracle test covering: single kth on 1-D input, multiple kth values, axis=None flattening behavior, axis=0/1 on 2-D input, duplicate elements with undefined interior ordering checked via partition invariants, and structured dtype order=... parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:12:22Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:35.743390585Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:35.741193881Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper delivered in commit 15c6698 (verified touches crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs and oracle test cases listed in bead acceptance)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-9w2r","title":"[PORT] numpy.polynomial.polynomial subtree flat wrappers (polyroots/polyfromroots/polypow)","description":"Close the last 3 MUST gaps in scripts/fnp_polynomial_compliance_matrix.py
output: polyfromroots, polyroots, polypow.

These names belong to numpy.polynomial.polynomial subpackage with
increasing-power coefficient convention (distinct from the top-level
legacy numpy.polyadd/polysub/polymul/polydiv decreasing-power
convention). Users currently reach them only via the crid-shipped
nested fnp_python.polynomial.polynomial.polyX path; there is no
flat-namespace fnp_python.polyfromroots() wrapper.

Acceptance:
- Three #[pyfunction] passthrough wrappers (polyroots, polyfromroots,
  polypow) routing to numpy.polynomial.polynomial.<same_name>.
- Matrix re-run reports MUST 66/66 = 100% (currently 63/66 = 95.5%).
- Parity oracle test polynomial_polynomial_subtree_wrappers_match_numpy
  asserts repr-exact output for each across 1-2 representative inputs.

Reference: numpy.polynomial.polynomial module, numpy 2.x docs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T13:35:28.170107241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T13:35:47.409451235Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
